sutta:
buddha said there are these 10 fattest monks that this is the translation for some yoga and these factors are things that bind us to the world it's like in chinese i think the translation is so it's like a saw it's like a something that's naked so what are the 10 letters the 5 factors leading to the lower world and the 5 leading to the higher world and what monks are the 5 leading to the lower world the first 1 is sakaya here they put the view of an individual group but nowadays some of the newer translations they put the view of a personality personality view but i prefer to translate it as the view of a body being or a being in the body i explain this afterwards second 1 is skeptical down clinging to rules and rituals number 4 is kama raga sensual desire or sensual craving and number 5 is malevolence these are the 5 leading to the lower world and what are the 5 leading to the higher world the first 1 is rupa raga craving for fine material existence the second 1 is craving for immaterial existence number these are the 5 factors leading to the higher world what do you mean by 5 factors leading to the lower world that means they lead us to the sensual realm or desire realm the desired realm is the realm where sensual desire or lust pervades all beings have this essential lust so the first if you translate as personality view on personality view it's like a view of a self yourself but then uh according to the sutras when a person attained sultana the first fruition first fruit area he has already destroyed sakaya deity he has destroyed sakaya deity but he still has a cell only the arahant does not have a cell even up to anagami they still have a cell so this cannot be something like a view of a cell to me the view of a body being is probably more correct because sakayam could be made out of 2 words why i say this because in some other suta the buddha said it is easier for a person to see that this body is not the cell it is very hard for us to see that there is no self even in the mind so to see that the body is not the self is easier hence i think that could see that this body is not the cell although for most people most ordinary people they think that this body is the self so that is why i think that the sakaya deity means that you can see that this body is not the cell but you still have a cell even [Music] associated with the cell but when you break this and then you see that the body is not the cell the second 1 is skeptical having doubt about the buddha dharma and sangha doubting that the buddha is enlightened without thinking that the dharma is the true teachings and having doubt about the sangha having done about the training having doubt whether this is wholesome or [Music] uh having doubt whether this is the proper path to walk this is not the proper path to work etc only when a person has has has some uh understanding of the dharma then he has no more doubt about the dhamma then the third 1 is so you you notice the first word is so this is clinging to rules and rituals so even rules you could cling to it so that is why the buddha said in some other that the vinaya is for us to cross over on the other shoulder suppose you needed to cross over to the other shore on the other back so you make a boat or something and once you have crossed over you don't still carry the boat on your head and you put the boat down and you walk without the boat so what the buddha is saying is that rules and rituals is has a certain purpose so we must know what is the purpose of the rules and rituals and don't cling to it if you cling to it then it becomes a show making a show of it instead of understanding what it is for and then the fourth 1 is essential craving essential [Music] the fifth 1 is these are the 5 factors that bring us to the lower world the world of sensual design and the desire realm and then the 5 higher factors are the first 1 is craving for fine material existence refers to the form realm the real 1 where those who attain jhana are reborn where the body is very fine that's why it's called fine material existence so some people like to be reborn then especially some people who don't understand the dhamma but they have attained jhana that is why some of those early indians they want to be to be reborn as this is craving for fine material existence second 1 is craving for immaterial existence arupa raga disgraving to be reborn in the arupa realm formless realm and then the third 1 is conceived mana still having that conceit conceit is here like a self still having that self and then the fourth 1 is restlessness and the fifth 1 is ignorance ignorance about the dhamma basically so out of these 10 factors if a person has become has attained the first path he has not destroyed any of the factors but in the sutas we find that normally a person attains the first path by listening to the dharma after listening to the dhamma and understanding the 4 noble truths and usually he has attained the first path then the buddha said such a person is an arya and before he dies he will that first path a day arya will have attained the first fruition before he dies automatically some some time or other before he dies he must attain the first fruit so when the person becomes a sota panda he destroys the first 3 factors in the view of a body being and then the second fruit area he would have destroyed this first tree as well as weaken sensual craving and weakened ill will via paga [Music] since this sensual breathing and they are 2 of the hindrances if if they are weakened it means probably means that the sakatakami has a certain degree of concentration not yet attained ghana but he has a certain degree of concentration which enables him to weaken the sensual craving and ill will [Music] view of a body being he would have destroyed skeptical doubt destroyed clinging to rules and rituals destroyed sensual craving and destroyed ill will that is third fruit even those leading to the higher world the arahant has destroyed


===== SOURCE: 088_Anguttara Nikaya Book 10C 1014 - 1017 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.txt =====
we are at the book of tencent today we start with anguttanikaya
